name: Sweet Potato-Currant Mini Bundt Cakes
recipeIngredient: 2 Jars (4-ounce) sweet potato baby food puree
recipeInstructions:
1. The sweet potato puree provides the moistness in these individual non-fat cakes.
2. Mini-bundt pans, each containing 6 bundt-shaped cups, are available in many cookware stores.
3. Remove the peel from oranges in large strips and remove any white pith on orange.
4. Finely chop the peel and currants together in a food processor.
5. Stir the chopped peel and currants into the juice from the oranges, sweet potato puree, milk, and egg substitute until combined.
6. Sift together cake flour, 1 cup sugar, baking soda, and salt.
7. Quickly stir the sweet potato mixture into the dry ingredients until just combined.
8. Pour the batter among 9 mini-bundt non-stick cups sprayed with non-stick vegetable spray.
9. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 20 to 25 minutes or until cakes are done.
10. Let cool in pan for 5 minutes before removing to a wire rack.
11. Combine remaining 1/3 cup sugar and cinnamon.
12. Roll the warm cakes in the sugar mixture.
Makes 9 mini-bundt cakes. Each mini-bundt cake contains about: 216 calories; 300 mg sodium; 0 cholesterol; 0 fat; 50 grams carbohydrates; 5 grams protein; 0.44 gram fiber.
